patch.insert_text: She also takes an oral tablet most days in spring.
added_claims: Patient takes an oral tablet (pill) most days in spring

reasons: The claim that the patient takes an oral tablet (pill) most days in spring is supported by the transcript span 'the pill most days in spring'; 'pill' reasonably equates to oral tablet.
No mention of this seasonal pill exists elsewhere in the note, so it is not redundant.
Patient-reported medication use belongs in the Subjective section, which already contains the medication review narrative.

patch.insert_text: She also continues a loratadine tablet for seasonal allergies, taken most days in spring.
added_claims: Patient continues loratadine tablet for seasonal allergies
Loratadine taken most days in spring

reasons: Evidence span only says 'the pill most days in spring'—it does not name loratadine or specify seasonal allergies, so both added claims are unsupported/invented.
Loratadine is not mentioned elsewhere in the note (only hydrocortisone and epinephrine), so the content is not redundant.
Subjective is an appropriate section for a home medication, but grounding fails.
